title: Implement conversation actor with participant linking and lifecycle
purpose: Implement the conversation actor — the supervised beamr process that mediates a structured exchange between parties. The conversation owns the exchange state (current phase, accumulated context, deadlines), links to participants via process links for immediate crash detection, and provides the ConversationHandle API for send/receive/close.
task: Build the conversation actor as a beamr process. The actor links to all participants and detects crashes immediately via EXIT signals (P3). Implement ConversationConfig (participants, timeout, durability mode) and ConversationHandle (send, receive, close). Implement the state machine tracking current phase and accumulated context. The conversation actor is supervised (C20). This brief covers the core lifecycle — conversation patterns (request-reply, streaming, dispatch) are LIM-005.

requirements.title: Implement conversation actor as a supervised beamr process
requirements.spec: The conversation actor SHALL be a beamr process spawned under a supervisor. The actor SHALL receive commands (Send, Receive, Close, QueryState) via its mailbox. IF the conversation actor crashes, THEN the supervisor SHALL restart it. The actor SHALL maintain its own state machine (phase, context).
acceptance: Conversation actor is spawned as a beamr process
Actor is supervised — crash triggers restart without affecting other conversations
Actor handles Send, Receive, Close, and QueryState commands
Actor maintains ConversationState with current_phase and accumulated context

files: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:1-1 — currently empty target for the supervised conversation actor and command loop.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/mod.rs:1-3 — currently declares only types and re-exports Conversation/ConversationMessage; add actor declaration/re-exports here, keeping mod.rs declaration-only.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:3-47 — existing tracing Conversation type; avoid replacing it accidentally when adding ConversationState/Handle/config types.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-17 — crate-root re-export style; no logic, only pub mod and pub use.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-16 — beamr dependency is present with json feature; do not add persistence/network dependencies for this brief.
context: The nearby channel actor/supervisor/registry files are empty, so there is no established in-repo actor-supervisor implementation to copy; this brief must create the first real convention for actor commands and restart behavior.
mod.rs files in this crate are deliberately only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports; keep conversation/mod.rs to that style.
Workspace lints deny unsafe code, panics, unwrap, expect, and clippy all/pedantic; actor code and tests must use Result paths instead of `todo!`, `unimplemented!`, or production panics.

beamr 0.6.4 exposes Scheduler spawn APIs (`spawn`, `spawn_trap_exit`, `spawn_link`) and process/link inspection helpers, but no high-level `Supervisor` type in the docs; supervisor behavior likely needs a small liminal wrapper that restarts one conversation PID when it exits.
scout.approach: Create actor.rs around an explicit public command protocol, e.g. `ConversationCommand::{Send, Receive, Close, QueryState}` plus a `ConversationActor`/supervisor wrapper that owns config, current state, and the beamr PID/handle needed to communicate with the actor. Spawn the actual actor as a beamr process where possible and wrap it in a liminal supervisor abstraction that records/restarts only that conversation; if direct BEAM entrypoint spawning is awkward in this scaffold, keep the public API shaped around PIDs/scheduler and write host-side tests with beamr scheduler test helpers while still proving a beamr process is spawned. Export the actor module and relevant public types from conversation/mod.rs, and make QueryState return a clone/snapshot of ConversationState for S13 diagnostics.
scout.notes: The biggest gotcha is beamr’s API level: docs emphasize VM primitives and Gleam OTP supervisors rather than a Rust `Supervisor` type. Avoid claiming supervision with a plain Rust struct only; tests should demonstrate crash/termination of one conversation process leads to a restarted conversation actor/PID without disturbing another conversation.

files_changed.note: Filled the empty actor target with ConversationCommand, ConversationSupervisor, ConversationActor, backend command processing, actor restart checks, and state query support.
Added the internal beamr module/NIF bridge that embodies each conversation as a beamr process with a mailbox receive loop.
Added typed queued command storage used by the mailbox wake command loop.
Added actor lifecycle, supervision, and linking tests while keeping actor.rs under the 500-line boundary.
Added actor module declaration and re-exported actor/supervisor/command types without adding logic.
Added ConversationState and phase/context types used by the actor state machine.

dev.how: ConversationSupervisor now installs a small internal beamr module and spawns each conversation via Scheduler::spawn_trap_exit. The spawned beamr process runs a mailbox receive loop; mailbox command wakeups call into a liminal NIF bridge that dispatches typed Send, Receive, Close, and QueryState commands against actor-owned state. The supervisor records the current actor PID and restarts just that conversation actor when a later command observes the prior PID has exited, leaving other conversations untouched. QueryState returns cloned ConversationState snapshots for diagnostics.
dev.deviation: The scouted plan called for typed commands via the actor mailbox; beamr’s public host enqueue API only supports immediate atom messages, so the implementation uses a mailbox atom as the command wakeup and a liminal-side typed queue for command payloads. The actor still receives and handles the command through its beamr mailbox loop, and EXIT signals are native beamr mailbox messages.

checklist.note: Conversation actors are beamr processes spawned by Scheduler::spawn_trap_exit under ConversationSupervisor, with tests proving actor PID creation and restart after termination.
ConversationState tracks current_phase and accumulated context; actor Send/Receive/Close/QueryState mutate or expose it.

stories.note: ConversationHandle::query_state and ConversationActor::state return cloned state snapshots for operator diagnostics of active conversations.

issues: Public ConversationHandle::query_state failed after normal close because ensure_running rejected Closed conversations; this weakened operator diagnostics for final states.
Actor file crossed the 500-line limit during hardening until helper functions were extracted.
fixes: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:289-296 now returns the final snapshot directly when the conversation is Closed.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/tests.rs:53-57 now verifies Closed lifecycle state through the public handle.query_state path.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/sync.rs:5-34 extracts reply/wait/lock helpers; final wc shows actor.rs 486 lines and all affected files below 500.

requirements.title: Implement participant linking for immediate crash detection
requirements.spec: WHEN a conversation is created, the conversation actor SHALL link to all participant processes. IF a participant process dies, THEN the conversation actor SHALL receive an EXIT signal on the same scheduler tick. The conversation actor SHALL handle the crash according to its configured policy (propagate failure, route to another participant, or compensate).
acceptance: Conversation actor links to each participant PID at creation time
Participant death triggers EXIT signal in the conversation actor
EXIT handler is invoked immediately (same scheduler tick, not after a timeout)
Test: participant process dies → conversation actor receives EXIT and handles it

files: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:1-1 — participant linking and EXIT handling belong in the actor creation/command loop here.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:1-88 — ConversationConfig participant field and CrashPolicy from R3 drive which PIDs actor.rs links and how EXIT is handled.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:24-34 — existing LiminalError variants include ConversationFailed, ConversationTimeout, and ParticipantCrashed for mapping EXIT/crash outcomes.
docs/design/core/design.json:17-19 — P3 requires same-tick process-link crash detection, no heartbeat/polling.
docs/design/core/checklist.json:56-61 — C20-C25 checklist items define conversation lifecycle/linking acceptance.
context: Use beamr process links, not timers or heartbeat loops. beamr docs expose `LinkFacility::link(caller_pid, target_pid) -> Result<(), LinkError>`, Scheduler `set_trap_exit(pid, true)`, `is_linked(left, right)`, and test helper `has_trapped_exit_message(target_pid, source_pid)`.

The conversation process should trap exits before linking participants so participant death becomes an `{EXIT, SourcePid, Reason}` mailbox message instead of killing the conversation actor by default.

CrashPolicy semantics in this brief should be minimal: `Fail` moves state to Failed/returns ParticipantCrashed; `RouteToNext` and `Compensate` can record policy decisions/state for later LIM-005/routing work rather than implement full rerouting/compensation.
Participant identity should be PID-like (`u64` or beamr ProcessHandle) and Debug/Clone; ProcessHandle is Copy/Clone/Send/Sync and exposes `pid()`, while raw Process is not Send/Sync.
scout.approach: During conversation creation, set trap-exit on the conversation actor PID, then link the actor to every configured participant PID via beamr’s link facility/scheduler-backed link path. Add an EXIT branch to the actor receive loop that marks the matching participant status dead and dispatches through `CrashPolicy`: fail the conversation immediately for `Fail`, mark a route-needed/compensation-needed failure context for the other policies without implementing routing or compensation patterns. Test with beamr scheduler test processes: create a trapping conversation actor, link inert participant(s), externally terminate a participant, assert `has_trapped_exit_message(conversation_pid, participant_pid)`/state transition happens without sleeping or timeout logic.
scout.notes: Same scheduler tick is hard to prove with wall-clock tests and should not be approximated with short timeouts. Prefer beamr’s direct trapped-exit inspection and absence of heartbeat/timer code; a sleep-based test would mask the key requirement and be flaky.

files_changed.note: Added boot-time participant linking, EXIT dispatch into crash-policy handling, and state updates for participant death.
Added EXIT tuple recognition in the beamr NIF bridge so trapped EXIT messages invoke participant crash handling.
Added participant death test using beamr process links and has_trapped_exit_message without sleeps or heartbeat timing.

dev.how: Actor processes are spawned with trap_exit enabled before they are runnable. During boot, the actor obtains beamr’s scheduler-backed LinkFacility from ProcessContext and links its PID to every configured participant PID. Participant termination produces a trapped {EXIT, SourcePid, Reason} mailbox message; the NIF bridge recognizes that tuple, marks the participant dead, records the policy decision, and applies CrashPolicy::Fail by moving the conversation to Failed while RouteToNext/Compensate remain recorded policy decisions for later routing/compensation work.

checklist.note: Boot links the conversation actor PID to each ConversationConfig participant via beamr LinkFacility::link.
Crash detection uses beamr process links/trapped EXIT messages only; no heartbeat, sleeps, or polling timeouts were added.

stories.note: Linked participant crashes are reflected immediately through trapped EXIT handling and queryable state.
The conversation can supervise worker PIDs via links; RouteToNext is recorded as a crash policy decision for later routing implementation without adding routing in this brief.

acceptance.criterion: Conversation actor links to each participant PID at creation time
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:355-377 links every config participant through beamr LinkFacility during boot; test conversation::actor::tests::actor_is_spawned_linked_and_queryable asserts scheduler.is_linked at crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/tests.rs:11-30.

acceptance.criterion: Participant death triggers EXIT signal in the conversation actor
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/beam.rs:124-138 routes non-command mailbox messages through exit_source; crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/beam.rs:146-153 recognizes {EXIT, SourcePid, Reason}; crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:455-477 handles the participant exit.

acceptance.criterion: EXIT handler is invoked immediately (same scheduler tick, not after a timeout)
acceptance.evidence: test conversation::actor::tests::participant_death_arrives_as_trapped_exit_without_timeout at crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/tests.rs:62-85 terminates the participant and immediately asserts has_trapped_exit_message before querying Failed state; no sleeps/heartbeats/timeouts are present in conversation actor code.

acceptance.criterion: Test: participant process dies → conversation actor receives EXIT and handles it
acceptance.evidence: conversation::actor::tests::participant_death_arrives_as_trapped_exit_without_timeout, crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/tests.rs:75-83: terminate_process, trapped EXIT assertion, Failed phase, and Dead participant health.

issues: CrashPolicy::Fail originally marked the state Failed but could leave already-pending receive callers blocked indefinitely.
Dev claim for S9 overstates this brief: immediate worker crash detection is present, but actual rerouting of work is explicitly deferred by the routing boundary.
fixes: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:455-477 now drains pending receives with LiminalError::ParticipantCrashed when Fail policy handles a participant EXIT.

requirements.title: Define ConversationConfig
requirements.spec: ConversationConfig SHALL specify: participant PIDs or handles, timeout (optional duration after which the conversation fails), durability mode (Ephemeral or Durable), and an on_participant_crash policy. ConversationConfig SHALL NOT have defaults for participants or policy — both are required.
acceptance: ConversationConfig defined in crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs
Fields: participants, timeout (Option<Duration>), mode (ChannelMode), on_crash (CrashPolicy)
CrashPolicy enum: Fail, RouteToNext, Compensate
ConversationConfig does not implement Default

files: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:1-88 — add ConversationConfig, CrashPolicy, participant status/state types here without deleting existing Conversation tracing helpers.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:4-12 — ChannelMode already defines Ephemeral/Durable and should be reused for conversation mode per acceptance.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-34 — ChannelConfig shows the current explicit-constructor/no-silent-default style to mirror for ConversationConfig.
crates/liminal/src/durability/config.rs:29-105 — explicit config constructor/accessor convention with validation and no implicit defaults.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:24-34 — existing conversation/participant error taxonomy for config validation failures or crash mapping if needed.
context: ConversationConfig must not implement Default; participants and on_crash are required. A direct `new(participants, timeout, mode, on_crash)` constructor matches ChannelConfig and is least likely to fail mechanical checks.
All public types need Debug; derive Clone/Debug/PartialEq/Eq where fields permit it. `Option<Duration>` and ChannelMode are Copy/Eq-friendly.

The brief says participant PIDs or handles. Given beamr `ProcessHandle` is Copy/Clone/Send/Sync with `pid()`, a small public `Participant`/`ParticipantPid(u64)` wrapper can decouple liminal from beamr internals while still allowing linking by PID.
Do not pull in haematite or durable-store types for `mode`; ChannelMode is already the requested Ephemeral/Durable marker and durable persistence is explicitly out of scope.
scout.approach: Extend conversation/types.rs with `CrashPolicy { Fail, RouteToNext, Compensate }`, a PID wrapper or alias used consistently by actor.rs, and `ConversationConfig { participants, timeout, mode, on_crash }`. Provide an all-fields-required constructor and accessor methods; validate empty participants if desired by returning LiminalError::ConversationFailed, but do not add Default or fallback policy. Keep existing Conversation/ConversationMessage tests passing by appending types rather than rewriting the file.
scout.notes: The prompt says `Files: create [conversation/types.rs]`, but the file already exists and contains tracing conversation helpers plus tests. Treat it as modify/extend; overwriting it would break existing behavior.

files_changed.note: Added ParticipantPid, CrashPolicy, ConversationConfig, participant status, and state support types.
Re-exported ConversationConfig, CrashPolicy, ParticipantPid, ParticipantStatus, and ParticipantHealth.

dev.how: ConversationConfig now contains the required participants, timeout, ChannelMode, and on_crash fields, with an all-fields constructor and no Default implementation. CrashPolicy defines Fail, RouteToNext, and Compensate; ParticipantPid wraps raw beamr PIDs while also accepting beamr ProcessHandle conversion.

checklist.note: ConversationConfig specifies participants, optional timeout, durability mode via ChannelMode, and required CrashPolicy; no Default implementation was added.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationConfig defined in crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:139-168 defines ConversationConfig and its all-fields constructor.

acceptance.criterion: Fields: participants, timeout (Option<Duration>), mode (ChannelMode), on_crash (CrashPolicy)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:141-149 contains participants, timeout: Option<Duration>, mode: ChannelMode, and on_crash: CrashPolicy.

acceptance.criterion: CrashPolicy enum: Fail, RouteToNext, Compensate
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:128-137 defines CrashPolicy::{Fail, RouteToNext, Compensate}.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationConfig does not implement Default
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:152-168 only implements the all-fields constructor; repository search for `impl Default for ConversationConfig` returned no matches.

requirements.title: Define ConversationHandle with send, receive, and close
requirements.spec: ConversationHandle SHALL expose send (deliver a message to the conversation), receive (wait for a message from the conversation via selective receive), and close (terminate the conversation normally). ConversationHandle SHALL be Clone + Send + Sync.
acceptance: ConversationHandle has send(&self, message) -> Result<(), LiminalError>
ConversationHandle has receive(&self) -> Result<Envelope, LiminalError> (or equivalent async)
ConversationHandle has close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError>
ConversationHandle is Clone + Send + Sync
ConversationHandle is re-exported from lib.rs

files: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:49-88 — existing ConversationMessage generic payload pattern; ConversationHandle can coexist below it and should not conflate tracing messages with actor envelopes.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:36-72 — ChannelHandle is the existing handle API style: Clone + Debug with Result-returning publish/subscribe/close stubs.
crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:1-9 — minimal cloneable handle stub style for public handles.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:14-17 — crate-root re-export block where ConversationHandle must be added.
crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:1-1 — crate-level Envelope is currently empty; receive needs either a new minimal Envelope here or an accepted equivalent type.
context: ConversationHandle must be Clone + Send + Sync; beamr MailboxSender is documented Clone/Debug/Send/Sync, while beamr Process is not Send/Sync, so the handle should store sender/PID/scheduler handles, not Process itself.

There is no existing crate-level Envelope despite lib.rs declaring the module. Either define a minimal Envelope in this brief if receive returns Envelope, or choose a clearly documented equivalent owned message type and re-export it if needed.

Public methods should return `Result<_, LiminalError>` and never panic on closed/dead actors; map failed delivery to DeliveryFailed or ConversationFailed, and closed state to ConversationFailed/ChannelClosed only if semantically clear.

Selective receive is a beamr mailbox concept, but Scheduler’s public host enqueue API is mostly atom-only; arbitrary messages may require an actor-command abstraction or a liminal-side channel until later protocol/envelope work lands.
scout.approach: Implement `ConversationHandle` as a cloneable wrapper around the conversation PID plus the mailbox/scheduler sending mechanism chosen in actor.rs. Add `send(&self, message: impl Into<Envelope or ConversationMessage>) -> Result<(), LiminalError>`, `receive(&self) -> Result<Envelope, LiminalError>` or an async/owned equivalent, and `close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError>` that sends corresponding ConversationCommand variants and maps delivery/closed errors. Re-export it from conversation/mod.rs and lib.rs, and add a compile-time test `fn assert_clone_send_sync<T: Clone + Send + Sync>() {}` for the handle.
scout.notes: If adding Envelope in envelope.rs, keep it minimal and Debug/Clone; full schema id/causal context/publisher/timestamp semantics are LIM-003/LIM-006 territory. Do not implement request-reply “single call” helpers here; LIM-005 owns pattern helpers.

files_changed.note: Added ConversationHandle with send, receive, close, query_state, and actor_pid operations backed by a Send+Sync trait object.
Implemented the handle backend against the supervised actor command path.
Added a minimal Debug+Clone local Envelope payload carrier for conversation send/receive.
Re-exported ConversationHandle, ConversationConfig, ConversationState, and Envelope from the crate root.

dev.how: ConversationHandle is Clone + Send + Sync by storing an Arc-backed backend, and exposes send, receive, and close methods returning Result<_, LiminalError>. send and close route typed commands through the actor mailbox wake path; receive performs selective-style actor receive by waiting on the conversation actor to either return the next queued Envelope or satisfy the receive when a later Send arrives. A minimal crate-level Envelope was added because the crate’s envelope module was empty and protocol/wire metadata is out of scope.
dev.deviation: The handle does not store beamr::MailboxSender directly because arbitrary host-to-process payload delivery is not exposed by beamr’s public scheduler API. It stores a Send+Sync backend around the supervised actor PID and command queue while preserving the required public send/receive/close API and mailbox-driven actor processing.

checklist.note: ConversationHandle exposes send, receive, and close; a compile-time test asserts Clone + Send + Sync.

stories.note: The handle hides inbox/correlation management behind a single conversation object; specific request-reply helpers remain deferred to LIM-005.
Agents can send an Envelope and synchronously receive the next Envelope through the same ConversationHandle, with pattern helpers deferred.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle has send(&self, message) -> Result<(), LiminalError>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:361-368 exposes send(&self, impl Into<Envelope>) -> Result<(), LiminalError>; backend dispatch is crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:112-115 and 268-273.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle has receive(&self) -> Result<Envelope, LiminalError> (or equivalent async)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:370-377 exposes receive -> Result<Envelope, LiminalError>; actor receive command submission is crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:275-280 and receive fulfillment/pending behavior is crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:396-428.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle has close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError>
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:379-386 exposes close -> Result<(), LiminalError>; close command submission/handling is crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:282-287 and 430-449.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle is Clone + Send + Sync
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:350-354 derives Clone and stores Arc<dyn ConversationHandleBackend>; crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:407-413 requires backend Debug + Send + Sync; test conversation::types::tests::conversation_handle_is_clone_send_sync passed.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationHandle is re-exported from lib.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:14-16 re-exports ConversationHandle from the crate root.

issues: Receive-side state errors could be returned as actor/NIF BADARG instead of through the waiting reply channel, which could make ConversationHandle::receive block instead of returning Result::Err.
Dev claims for S4/S8 overstate this brief: full request-reply and same-call synchronous helpers are specific conversation patterns deferred to LIM-005 by the boundary.
fixes: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:396-428 now sends lock/transition errors through the receive reply channel and only parks a receive after successful state activation.

requirements.title: Implement conversation state machine
requirements.spec: The conversation actor SHALL track its state as a ConversationState containing: current phase (Created, Active, Completing, Closed, Failed), accumulated context (Vec of exchanged messages or references), deadline (optional Instant), and participant status (alive/dead per participant).
acceptance: ConversationState tracks phase, context, deadline, and participant status
Phase transitions are explicit: Created → Active → Completing → Closed; any state → Failed
State is queryable via QueryState command
Test: conversation progresses through Created → Active → Closed lifecycle

files: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:1-88 — define ConversationState, ConversationPhase, ParticipantStatus, and context entry/message reference types here.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:1-1 — enforce phase transitions and QueryState handling in the actor loop.
crates/liminal/src/aion/types.rs:14-36 — Aion activity request/result shows future conversation payload use, but R5 should store opaque context, not Aion-specific workflow logic.
docs/design/core/design.json:5-6 — design states conversations own current phase, accumulated context, deadlines, and participant links; durable resume is a future persistence concern.
docs/design/core/briefs/LIM-005.json:24-30 — request-reply/streaming/dispatch semantics are explicitly next brief, so R5 should stop at generic lifecycle transitions.
context: ConversationState acceptance requires `current_phase`, accumulated context, optional `Instant` deadline, and alive/dead participant status. Use `Instant` for in-memory deadline; do not serialize or persist it.
Phase transitions must be explicit: Created -> Active -> Completing -> Closed, and any state -> Failed. Implement helper methods returning Result instead of open-coded mutation throughout actor.rs.
QueryState should return a snapshot clone so operators/tests can inspect state without sharing mutable actor internals.
Durable conversations in ADR-006 are about future event-sourced processing state; for this brief `ChannelMode::Durable` should be stored in config/state but not written to haematite.
scout.approach: Add state types in conversation/types.rs: `ConversationPhase`, `ConversationState`, a participant status map/list keyed by participant PID, and a lightweight context entry type such as stored Envelope/message references. Implement transition helpers (`activate`, `begin_completing`, `close`, `fail`) that validate allowed paths and update `current_phase`. In actor.rs, initialize Created with deadline = timeout.map(|d| Instant::now() + d), transition to Active on first Send/Receive, Completing/Closed on Close, Failed on crash/timeout/error, append exchanged messages to context, and service QueryState by returning the cloned snapshot.
scout.notes: Watch file length: conversation/types.rs already has tests and may grow quickly; if approaching 500 lines, move actor-only command/tests into actor.rs and keep mod.rs re-export-only. Also avoid deriving Eq for structs containing Instant if it becomes awkward; Debug is mandatory, exact Eq is not.

files_changed.note: Added ConversationPhase, ConversationState, ConversationContextEntry, ParticipantStatus, ParticipantHealth, deadline, transition helpers, and context recording helpers.
Applied explicit Created -> Active -> Completing -> Closed transitions and Failed transitions on crash policy.
Added tests for Created -> Active -> Closed lifecycle and participant crash failure transition.

dev.how: ConversationState is initialized from ConversationConfig with phase Created, optional Instant deadline derived from timeout, participant liveness entries, accumulated context, and retained ChannelMode. Actor Send/Receive activate the conversation and append Sent/Received context; Close explicitly activates if needed, moves through Completing, then Closed; participant failure records dead status and moves to Failed for CrashPolicy::Fail. QueryState returns a clone of the state snapshot.

checklist.note: The state machine tracks current phase, accumulated context, optional deadline, durability mode, and alive/dead participant status with explicit transition helpers.

stories.note: Within LIM-004 boundaries, durable mode and in-flight state are represented in memory for future durable resume; haematite persistence and server-restart replay are intentionally not implemented per the brief’s SHALL NOT boundary.

acceptance.criterion: ConversationState tracks phase, context, deadline, and participant status
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:235-248 defines current_phase, context, deadline, participants, and mode; crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:253-269 initializes deadline and alive participant status from ConversationConfig.

acceptance.criterion: Phase transitions are explicit: Created → Active → Completing → Closed; any state → Failed
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:276-285 implements Created/Active activation, 292-300 Active→Completing, 308-317 Completing→Closed, and 319-322 any state→Failed.

acceptance.criterion: State is queryable via QueryState command
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:289-296 submits QueryState or returns the final Closed snapshot; crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:350 handles QueuedCommandKind::QueryState; crates/liminal/src/conversation/types.rs:388-395 exposes handle.query_state.

acceptance.criterion: Test: conversation progresses through Created → Active → Closed lifecycle
acceptance.evidence: test conversation::actor::tests::handle_progresses_created_active_closed_lifecycle at crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor/tests.rs:32-60 asserts Created, Active after send, and Closed after close through the public handle.

issues: The original lifecycle test verified Closed state through a private actor.core snapshot rather than the public QueryState path, missing an operator-diagnostics failure after close.
apply_send popped a pending receive before validating the phase transition, so a Failed/Closed transition error could orphan a waiting receiver.

Dev claim for S11 overstates this brief: durable restart/replay across server restart is explicitly outside LIM-004; the implementation only retains mode/deadline/state in memory for later durability work.
fixes: crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:289-296 makes final Closed state queryable through ConversationHandle::query_state.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/actor.rs:380-393 validates activation and records Sent before popping/delivering to a pending receive.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement specific conversation patterns (request-reply, streaming, dispatch) — those are LIM-005.
SHALL NOT implement durable conversation persistence — that is the durability cluster.
SHALL NOT implement conversation routing — that is the routing cluster.
SHALL NOT implement the wire protocol for remote conversations — that is the protocol cluster.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
Conversation actor is a beamr process, not a Rust struct.
Participant crash detection is via process linking, not heartbeat or timeout.
